description_long: This house features a simple, single-story structure with a prominent gable roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a light, off-white color with dark trim and a visible wooden base, giving it a modest, utilitarian appearance. The front facade includes two rectangular windows with brown frames and a covered porch area supported by posts, providing a sheltered entry. The rear side has a single door and minimal window openings, emphasizing privacy and functionality. The roof extends over a carport or sheltered area on one side, supported by wooden beams and a wooden deck floor. The overall design is practical, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on straightforward residential use.
description: A single-story house with a gray gable roof, light walls, brown trim, and a covered porch with a carport. The design is simple and functional, with rectangular windows and minimal decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing two rectangular windows, brown trim, and a covered porch area with a carport to the left.
description_view_1: Rear view featuring a single door, no windows on the visible wall, and the intersection of two gable roof sections.
description_view_2: Side view displaying a long, uninterrupted wall with two doors and a small window, emphasizing the house's length.
description_view_3: Side view with two small windows and a clear view of the carport's wooden deck and supporting posts.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with a simple door, supported by posts and an overhanging roof, no decorative elements.
